data.title: First Watch gains after saying at ICR that its restaurant pipeline is strong
data.description:
First Watch Restaurant Group (FWRG) rallied on Monday after providing a business update at the ICR Conference. 

The restaurant company said Q4 same-restaurant sales were up +3.1% and full-year same-restaurant sales rose 3.6%. Same-restaurant growth was down 1.9% in Q4, but was 0.5% higher for the full year. On the development front, First Watch (FWRG) highlighted that it had 13 new system-wide restaurant openings in Q4, consisting of 12 company-owned restaurants and one franchise-owned restaurant. For the full year, there were 64 system-wide new restaurant openings (55 company-owned and nine franchise-owned) and three closures. As of December 28, First Watch (FWRG) had 633 system-wide restaurants, consisting of 560 company-owned restaurants and 73 franchise-owned restaurants across 32 states.

CEO Chris Tomasso noted that the 2024 and 2025 new restaurant classes combined continue to outperform the comparable restaurant base and the company's own underwriting expectations. Tomasso noted that the pipeline for 2026 is strong. "We remain confident in the continued execution of our long-term strategy and extending our lead in daytime dining," added.

Shares of First Watch (FRWG) were up 3.3% in afternoon trading.

data.title: SA Asks: Does Powell DOJ probe mark end of Fed independence? (Update)
data.description:
Does the announcement of a Justice Department probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell following months of pressure by the Trump administration to lower interest rates mark the end of the central bank's independence?

Seeking Alpha analysts Sagar Agarwal, APAC Investment News, Geneva Investor, Eugenio Catone, and Julia Ostian weigh in. 

Sagar Agarwal: Challenging the Fed’s independence is easier said than done, primarily because the Fed’s policy actions are more public than ever before. The implication is that if the Fed were to act in a manner incongruent with prevailing economic conditions, market participants would interpret this as a loss of Fed credibility, triggering negative price reactions across all asset classes and possibly pushing the global economy into a deep and severe recession. Such an outcome would be detrimental to the ambitions of any political figure who may have orchestrated a closer coupling between the Fed and the government.

Additionally, while all seven Fed board governors, including the chair, are appointed by the sitting president of the U.S., the remaining five votes belong to the president of the New York Fed and four other regional Fed presidents selected through a rotation system. If these five voters were to believe that the Fed’s independence was being challenged, they could be publicly vocal about it—much as Chair Powell was this Sunday. 

APAC Investment News: Unfortunately, I don't believe Federal Reserve independence is especially high on President Trump's to-care-about list. I believe he will erode as much independence as possible in pursuit of his goals. Ultimately, the president's limits will likely come down to what Congress and/or the Supreme Court decide to pursue.

Geneva Investor: I don't think we are seeing the end of the Fed's independence just yet. On the contrary, I see this as Powell's last attempt at safeguarding the Fed's independence by putting his reputation on the line. However, with Powell having only four months left as Fed chair and mounting political pressure, this may not matter. Markets may be already starting to price in a "new" Fed, which, even if not fully dependent on the government, may become more easily swayed.

Eugenio Catone: In my opinion, the federal investigation into Jerome Powell is a concerning event that could set a dangerous precedent for the U.S. economy. If the central bank is no longer independent, this would mean not acting in the best interest of the country but instead favoring short-term political agendas. 

Therefore, the U.S. dollar could depreciate even further, and the 10Y bond yield could surge, despite a lower Fed Funds Rate. This divergence between the short end and the long end of the yield curve reflects all the doubts of bond vigilantes. For a net importer country such as the U.S., a further weakening of the U.S. dollar could mean upward pressure on the inflation rate.

Julia Ostian: I view this more as political theater than a structural collapse of the Fed. The Federal Reserve has survived intense political pressure before, and its independence remains intact. While headlines generate volatility, I believe the institution will continue to prioritize data over politics, though the noise level will undeniably make their communication job harder.

The biggest risk, in my opinion, is psychological. The market is quick to judge, so if investors perceive political pressure behind rate decisions, we could see inflation expectations unanchor and yields spike regardless of the Fed's actual intent.

(Updated to add commentary by Julia Ostian.)

data.title: Anthropic unveils new Cowork tool to make Claude Code more accessible
data.description:
Anthropic (ANTHRO) unveiled its new Cowork tool on Monday, in an effort to make its Claude Code coding tool more accessible.

Available right now in research preview, Cowork is available to subscribers of Anthropic's Claude Max via its macOS app, Anthropic said. The tool allows users to point to a specific folder that Claude will be able to read or edit via a chat interface. It should allow for coding tasks to be done as simply as having a chat with coworkers, Anthropic added.

“In Cowork, Claude completes work like this with much more agency than you’d see in a regular conversation,” the company said in a release, accompanying the news. “Once you’ve set it a task, Claude will make a plan and steadily complete it, while looping you in on what it’s up to. If you’ve used Claude Code, this will feel familiar—Cowork is built on the very same foundations. This means Cowork can take on many of the same tasks that Claude Code can handle, but in a more approachable form for non-coding tasks.”

Claude Code was launched in November 2024, and it has become one of Anthropic's more successful product launches. Anthropic is on the verge of raising $10B in a new funding round that would value the generative AI company at $350B.

data.title: Revolution Medicines declines to comment on M&A speculation at conference
data.description:
• Revolution Medicines (RVMD) declined to comment on recent M&A speculation at the JPMorgan Healthcare conference in San Francisco.
  • Revolution Medicines (RVMD) cited a company policy of not commenting on rumors or speculation at the conference on Monday.
  • The no comments come after reports last week that Merck (MRK) was in talks to purchase Revolution (RVMD). The WSJ reported on Friday that Merck was in discussions to acquire the cancer-drug biotech for about $30 billion.  A deal for Revolution could come as soon as later this month. Merck was discussing a deal valued between $28 billion and $32 billion.
  • Shares of Revolution (RVMD) edged lower by 0.5% on Monday after surging 50% last week on deal speculation.

data.title: American Water Works raised at BofA as merger approval faces long path but addresses bear case
data.description:
American Water Works (AWK) +2.1% in Monday's trading as Bank of America upgraded the utility to Neutral from Underperform with a $139 price target, raised from $117, as shares trade at a trough valuation spread relative to electric and gas utilities, with its P/E premium compressing from more than 140% historically to less than 25% currently.

BofA analyst Ross Fowler believes the pending merger with Essential Utilities could help address concerns around water bill affordability and the sustainability of earnings growth guidance, and the company's 50–100-year infrastructure replacement cycle should warrant a valuation premium compared to electric peers, which exhibit similar ~9% EPS growth but depend more upon rapid infrastructure buildouts to serve a concentrated base of commercial data center customers.

Fowler said prior concerns have focused on three areas - electric and gas utility EPS growth has caught up to American Water Works (AWK), eroding the historical 20%-40% premium that was previously supported by 200-300 bps higher growth; the company relies on frequent rate hikes across multiple jurisdictions to achieve its 7%-9% EPS compound annual growth rate guidance, but policymakers have raised affordability concerns, most notably in the recent Pennsylvania rate case; and the long-duration investment profile and premium valuation historically have lagged during periods of rising interest rates, with the 140% peak premium occurring during the near-zero rate environment of the COVID era - and he believe these risks have largely played out, are significantly priced in, and could be mitigated if the merger is approved.

data.title: Bessent told Trump that DOJ's Powell probe 'made a mess' - report
data.description:
An irate Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent reportedly told President Donald Trump late Sunday that the Justice Department criminal probe into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell "made a mess" and could hurt the financial markets, according to a media report on Monday.

Bessent wasn't just worried about financial markets, two people familiar with the matter told Axios. He's now concerned that Powell may be more likely to stay on the Federal Reserve Board as a governor until his term ends in January 2028. His term as chair ends in May of this year.

The Treasury secretary "thought that when the president named a new Fed chair, that Powell would go. But now that's not going to happen," one source told Axios. "Now [Powell is] dug in. This really made a mess of things."

Bloomberg earlier reported that Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Bill Pulte was thought to be instrumental in urging the DOJ to investigate Powell, but Pulte denied having a role in federal prosecutors issuing the subpoenas, according to an interview with Bloomberg. He said the investigation is "outside of my purview" and directed any questions to the Justice Department.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt also said that the president didn't direct the DOJ to launch the probe.

While Leavitt said the president didn't order the DOJ to start the investigation, in late December he threatened to sue Powell for "gross incompetence." 

In trading on Monday, the dollar fell 0.3% against the euro (USD:EUR) and 0.5% against the British pound (USD:GBP). In the bond market, the slide in U.S. Treasuries pared losses incurred earlier in the day. In midafternoon trading, the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.83% after touching nearly 4.87% earlier in the session. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) edged up 1 bp to 4.18%, down from its session high of 4.21%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Big night for Warner Bros Discovery at 83rd Golden Globes
data.description:
The 83rd Golden Globes delivered a notable night for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) with the studio claiming top honors across multiple categories, including best dramatic motion picture and cinematic and box office achievement.

Hosted by the Hollywood Foreign Press Association, the Golden Globes – which kicks off the 2026 awards season – honors excellence in both film and TV. And while nominees were sprinkled across big Hollywood studios like Warner Bros (WBD) and Paramount Pictures (PSKY), smaller, niche studios made a very noticeable presence, notably NEON Films with 10 nominations including Best Motion Picture for “It Was Just an Accident,” “The Secret Agent,” “Sentimental Value,” “No Other Choice,” and “Arco,” for best animated film.   

“One Battle After Another” not only describes the high-stakes contest between Netflix (NFLX) and Paramount Skydance (PSKY) for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) but also scored the top prize for Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy for the studio. Warner Bros (WBD) also took home top honors for Cinematic and Box Office Achievement and Best Original Score for “Sinners,” as well as acting, directing, and screenplay nods for “One Battle After Another.”  In the TV categories, Warner Bros. Discovery’s (WBD) “The Pitt” won best TV series and best actor, and best actress for “Hacks.”

Focus Features (CMCSA) also had a big night with “Hamnet” winning for both Best Motion Picture – Drama, and Best Performance by a Lead Actress.

Netlix’s (NFLX) “Adolescence” won four Globes – the most of any TV show – for best actor, best supporting actress, best supporting actor, and best limited series, while the viral   “KPop Demon Hunters” took the top prize for best animated motion picture and best original song. Rival Apple TV only pulled in two awards, both for “The Studio,”

And despite box-office blockbusters “Zootopia 2” and “Avatar: Fire and Ash,” Disney (DIS) lost out to Netflix’s “KPop” in the animated and best original song categories, and to “Sinners” in cinematic and box office achievement.

data.title: Meta Platforms unveils Meta Compute as it scales up in the AI infrastructure arms race
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced on Monday that the company plans to establish a new "top-level" initiative called Meta Compute focused on building tens of gigawatts of AI infrastructure this decade and hundreds of gigawatts or more over time.

Meta Compute will be led by Santosh Janardhan, who will continue to oversee data center technical architecture, software stack, silicon programs, and operation of the global fleet and backbone network.

The early reaction from analysts is that the initiative is a foundational, multi-decade platform bet from the company on how effectively it engineers and finances compute capacity to position itself in the AI arms race.

Shares of Meta Platforms (META) were down 1.0% in Monday afternoon trading.

data.title: NATO seeks early Turkish F-16 deployment for Baltic air patrols
data.description:
NATO asked Turkey to deploy F-16 fighter jets to the Baltic Air Policing mission in 2026, advancing Ankara’s participation by several months, Bloomberg News reported Monday, citing people familiar with the discussions.

Under the proposal, Turkish aircraft would carry out patrols from Estonia for four months starting in August and running through December. The request reflects heightened alliance vigilance following repeated Russian airspace violations in the Baltic region.

Turkey had already been scheduled to contribute aircraft to NATO’s air policing effort in Romania from December 2026 through March 2027, the people said. Ankara has yet to decide whether it will accept the earlier deployment.

Turkish jets last flew Baltic air patrols in early 2025. The country’s defense ministry declined to comment on the latest request, while NATO did not immediately respond to inquiries.

The issue comes as the government of President Recep Tayyip Erdogan prepares to host the NATO leaders’ summit in July.

Security concerns have intensified for Turkey in recent months. In December, a Turkish F-16 shot down an unmanned aerial vehicle over the Black Sea as it neared Turkish airspace. Days later, a drone believed to be of Russian origin was discovered in northwestern Turkey, underscoring Ankara’s sensitivity to spillover risks from the conflict region.

data.title: Best and worst-rated stocks heading into earnings
data.description:
With the fourth quarter earnings season approaching, Seeking Alpha generated a list of best- and worst-rated mid- to mega-cap stocks as per its Quant model.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

In the week ahead, 14 S&P 500 companies—including two Dow 30 members—are set to report fourth-quarter earnings. FactSet estimates year-over-year earnings growth for the S&P 500 at 8.3% in Q4, which, if realized, would mark the index’s 10th straight quarter of earnings growth.

Here’s a look at the top-rated stocks as per Seeking Alpha’s quant model:

  • Micron Technology (MU), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • Lumentum Holdings (LITE), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • Nuvation Bio (NUVB), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • New Gold (NGD), Quant Rating 4.99 – Strong Buy.
  • Sibanye Stillwater (SBSW), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • Ciena (CIEN), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • Applied Digital (APLD), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • CommScope (COMM), Quant Rating 4.98 – Strong Buy.
  • nLIGHT (LASR), Quant Rating 4.97 – Strong Buy.
  • Lundin Mining (LUNMF), Quant Rating 4.97 – Strong Buy.

Here are the lowest-rated stocks as per Seeking Alpha’s quant model:

  • Fermi (FRMI), Quant Rating 1.00 – Strong Sell.
  • Ørsted (DNNGY), Quant Rating 1.03 – Strong Sell.
  • Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical (RARE), Quant Rating 1.05 – Strong Sell.
  • Lucid Group (LCID), Quant Rating 1.05 – Strong Sell.
  • Corcept Therapeutics (CORT), Quant Rating 1.06 – Strong Sell.
  • Trex (TREX), Quant Rating 1.09 – Strong Sell.
  • Telix Pharmaceuticals (TLX), Quant Rating 1.10 – Strong Sell.
  • Bitdeer Technologies (BTDR), Quant Rating 1.11 – Strong Sell.
  • BellRing Brands (BRBR), Quant Rating 1.13 – Strong Sell.
  • Zai Lab (ZLAB), Quant Rating 1.13 – Strong Sell.

Looking at sectors, Industrials (XLI), Technology (XLK), and Materials (XLB) were the highest-rated (“Buy”) among the 11 S&P 500 (SP500) sectors, with a Quant score of 4.12, 4.10, and 4.03, respectively. Utilities (XLU) was rated Hold, while Consumer Staples (XLP) and Real Estate (XLRE) received Strong Sell ratings.

As per FactSet, eight of the 11 S&P 500 sectors are projected to report year-over-year growth, led by the information technology sector. On the other hand, three sectors are predicted to report a year-over-year decline in earnings, led by the Consumer Discretionary sector.

Here is a list of ETFs to track the broader U.S. market: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).
